title: Chocolate Chip Muffins
description: Soft, moist muffins made without eggs or dairy. Makes about 10-12 muffins.
ingredients: 1 cup mini vegan chocolate chips (170 g)
steps: Preheat oven to 350°F.
Mix the flour, baking powder, and salt. Set aside.
Add wet ingredients and gently combine. Make sure not to over mix the batter!
Gently fold in chocolate chips.
Fill muffin tins to the top, sprinkle extra chips, and bake for around 20 mins, or until an inserted toothpick comes out clean!

title: Double Chocolate Chip Cookies
description: Chewy and chocolatey vegan cookies with a rich cocoa base. These are honestly probably the only cookie I have ever perfected and they come out amazing every. single. time. Makes about 16-18 cookies.
ingredients: 1/2 tsp espresso powder (optional, but highly recommended)
1 cup (170 g) vegan chocolate chips or however many your heart desires :)
steps: Before you start, I highly recommend using a kitchen scale for measuring out the ingredients!
In a bowl, whisk together flour, cocoa powder, baking soda, salt, and espresso powder. Set this bowl aside.
In a separate bowl, cream the softened vegan butter with brown sugar and granulated sugar for around 3 minutes using a mixer. Slowly add in the vanilla and apple sauce until well combined.
Gradually add the dry ingredients to the wet, mixing with a wooden spoon gently until a dough forms.
Fold in the chocolate chips or chunks. I recommend using 2 types for a nice variety, Trader Joes has an excellent selection.
Optional: Put the dough in the fridge for around 2 hours. This step isn't necesarry, but I've noticed the cookies do come out more flavorful and gooey when refigerated before baking.
Scoop the cookie dough onto the baking sheet, roughly the size of 2 tablespoons per cookie and bake for 10–12 minutes, or until you can tell they are done!
Let cookies cool for about 10 minutes and then enjoy :)

title: GF Chocolate Chip Cookies
description: Soft and chewy chocolate chip cookies made gluten-free with oat flour! I make these for my gluten-free friends and they absolutely adore them! Most can't even tell they are gluten free besides the subtle oat taste, but that could easily be replaced with 1:1 gluten free flour, I just like using the oat flour tbh. Makes about 16-18 cookies.
ingredients: 1 tbsp (10 g) arrowroot starch (or corn/potato starch)
1/2 cup (113 g) salted vegan butter, melted
1 cup (170 g) vegan chocolate chips or however many your heart desires :)
steps: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C) and line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
In a medium bowl, whisk together oat flour, arrowroot starch, baking powder, and baking soda.
In a separate bowl, cream together the melted vegan butter, granulated sugar, and brown sugar for about 3 minutes.
Stir in the dairy-free yogurt and vanilla extract into the sugar/butter mixture until combined.
Gradually mix the dry ingredients into the wet ingredients to form a dough. I recommend using a wooden spoon.
Fold in chocolate chips or chunks.
Optional: Put the dough in the fridge for around 2 hours. This step isn't necesarry, but I've noticed the cookies do come out more flavorful and gooey when refigerated before baking.
Scoop the cookie dough onto the baking sheet, roughly the size of 2 tablespoons per cookie and bake for 10–12 minutes, or until edges are golden brown.
Let cookies cool for about 10 minutes and then enjoy :)

title: Chocolate Chip Cookies
description: Chewy vegan chocolate chip cookies made with no eggs. It took me about 10 years to master this chocolate chip cookie recipe. I have made so many modifications it's not even funny. However, these take the cake for now, but I trust I will come up with a new and improved recipe soon :) Makes about 20-24 cookies.
ingredients: 2/3 cup (150 g) salted vegan butter, softened
steps: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C) and line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
In a medium bowl, whisk together flour, cornstarch, baking soda, and salt. Set aside.
In a large bowl, beat the softened vegan butter with brown sugar and granulated sugar until fluffy.
Add applesauce and vanilla extract to the mixture and whisk to combine.
Slowly add the dry ingredients into the wet mixture until a dough forms. I recommend using a wooden spoon. GENTLY COMBINE!
Fold in the chocolate chips.
Put the dough in the freezer for around 30 minutes. This step isn't necesarry, but I've noticed the cookies do come out more flavorful and gooey when cooled before baking.
Scoop the cookie dough onto the baking sheet, roughly the size of 2 tablespoons per cookie and bake for 10–12 minutes, or until until edges are golden brown.
Let cookies cool for about 10 minutes and then enjoy :)

title: Funfetti Sugar Cookies
description: Chewy in the middle, crispy on the outside, fun sugar cookies! Makes about 16-18 cookies.
ingredients: 1/4 cup (60 g) applesauce (or ~60 g dairy-free yogurt)
steps: Preheat oven to 350°F.
Mix the flour, baking powder, baking soda, cornstarch, salt, sprinkles in a medium-sized bowl. Set aside.
Cream the melted butter, sugar, applesauce, and vanilla until smooth, no clumps. For about 3 minutes.
Add the wet ingredients to the dry and gently combine. Make sure not to over mix the batter! I recommend using a wooden spoon.
Fold in sprinkles, however many you would like honestly.
Optional: Put the dough in the fridge for around 2 hours. This step isn't necesarry, but I've noticed the cookies do come out more flavorful and gooey when refigerated before baking.
Scoop the cookie dough onto the baking sheet, roughly the size of 2 tablespoons per cookie and bake for 10–12 minutes, or until edges are golden brown.
Let cookies cool for about 10 minutes and then enjoy :)

title: Ginger Snap Cookies
description: Chewy in the middle, crispy on the outside, fun ginger cookies! Makes about 28-32 cookies.
ingredients: 1 cup (200 g) white sugar for rolling
steps: Preheat oven to 350°F, line 2 baking sheets with parchment paper.
Mix the flour, baking soda, salt, ginger, cinnamon, and nutmeg in a medium-sized bowl. Set aside.
Cream the softened butter, brown sugar, molasses, and vanilla until smooth, no clumps. For around 3 minutes.
Add wet ingredients and gently combine. Make sure not to over mix the batter! I recommend using a wooden spoon.
Roll the dough into desired balls in the white sugar, and bake for around 10-12 minutes depending on size of cookies. Until the edges are golden brown.

title: Linzer Tart Cookies
description: Fun Holiday Linzer Tart Cookies! Makes about 18-22 sandwich cookies.
steps: Preheat oven to 350°F, line 2 baking sheets with parchment.
Mix the flour and almond meal together, set aside.
Cream the softened butter, sugar, salt, lemon-zest, yogurt, and vanilla until smooth, no clumps.
Add wet ingredients and gently combine. Make sure not to over mix the batter!
Roll the dough to 1/8 thickness and refrigerate for 20 minutes.
Begin to cut shapes, half of the shapes plain and half of them with a shape cut out in the center. Bake for about 8-10 minutes or until golden brown.
Once cookies are cooled, add the jam to the center, sandwich the cookie, and dust with powdered sugar!

title: S'mores Cookies
description: Fun S'mores Cookies! Makes about 18-20 cookies.
ingredients: 1/4 cup (57 g) applesauce or dairy-free yogurt
1 1/2 cups (255 g) chocolate chips or chopped chocolate
steps: Preheat oven to 350°F, line 2 baking sheets with parchment.
Mix the flour, salt, baking soda, and set aside.
Cream the butter, sugar, yogurt/applesauce, and vanilla until smooth, no clumps.
Add wet ingredients and gently combine. Make sure not to over mix the batter!
Fold in the chocolate chips and graham crackers
Roll the dough into desired balls, make a cavity in the middle, and place marshmallow. Bake for around 10-15 mins depending on size of cookies. Until the edges are golden brown.

title: Valentines Day Cookies
description: Valentines Day Cookies! Makes about 12-16 cut-out cookies depending on cutter size.
ingredients: 6 tbsp oil (82 g) / melted vegan butter (84 g)
steps: Preheat oven to 325°F, line 2 baking sheets with parchment.
Mix the flour, salt, baking soda, baking powder, and set aside.
Mix the oil/butter, sugar, and vanilla until smooth.
Add wet ingredients and gently combine. Make sure not to over mix the batter!
Roll the dough to about 1/4 inch thick and cut into shapes. Bake for around 10-12 minutes until golden brown. Add icing if desired!

title: Iced Lemon Cookies
description: Chewy vegan chocolate chip cookies made with no eggs—just pure cookie magic. Makes about 20-24 cookies.
ingredients: 1 tbsp cornstarch or arrowroot starch or potato starch
2/3 cup (150 g) salted vegan butter, softened
1/3 cup (85 g) unsweetened applesauce, room temperature
1 cup (170 g) vegan chocolate chips (semi-sweet or your choice!)
steps: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C) and line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
In a medium bowl, whisk together flour, cornstarch, baking soda, and salt.
In a large bowl, beat the softened vegan butter with brown sugar and granulated sugar until fluffy.
Add applesauce and vanilla extract to the wet mixture and stir to combine.
Slowly add the dry ingredients into the wet mixture until a dough forms.
Fold in the chocolate chips.
Scoop dough onto the prepared baking sheet and bake for 10–12 minutes until edges are golden.
Cool on the tray for 5 minutes before transferring to a cooling rack.

title: Iced Oatmeal Cookies
description: Chewy vegan chocolate chip cookies made with no eggs—just pure cookie magic. Makes about 18-20 cookies.
ingredients: 1/2 cup (113 g) salted vegan butter, melted
1/3 cup (85 g) unsweetened applesauce, room temperature
steps: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C) and line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
In a medium bowl, whisk together flour, cornstarch, baking soda, and salt.
In a large bowl, beat the vegan butter with brown sugar and granulated sugar until fluffy.
Add applesauce and vanilla extract to the wet mixture and stir to combine.
Slowly add the dry ingredients into the wet mixture until a dough forms.
Let chill for 30 minutes.
Scoop dough onto the prepared baking sheet and bake for 10–12 minutes until edges are golden.
Cool on the tray for 5 minutes before transferring to a cooling rack.

title: Bakery-Style Berry Muffin
description: Amazing Vegan Bakery-Style Berry Muffin! Makes about 12 muffins.
ingredients: 3 : 2 : 1 ratio of flour, brown sugar, and cubed butter
powdered sugar & splash of plant-based milk
steps: Preheat oven to 425°F.
Mix the flour, sugar, baking powder, and salt. Set aside.
Add wet ingredients and gently combine. Make sure not to over mix the batter!
Fill muffin tins halfway, sprinkle some berries (I prefer frozen), fill rest, and place rest of berries on top.
Make streusel in a seperate bowl and add to the top of each muffin before baking. Totally optional, but makes a nice crispy sweet muffin top!
Bake for around 20 mins, or until an inserted toothpick comes out clean!
Wait around 30 minutes for muffins to cool, then add the icing on top for an extra touch of sweetness!

title: Blueberry Muffins
description: Simple Vegan Blueberry Muffins! Makes about 12 muffins.
ingredients: 1 tsp cider or white wine vinegar (or lemon juice)
1 1/4 cups fresh or frozen blueberries (180 g)
steps: Preheat the oven to 400°F. Line a muffin tin with paper liners.
In a large bowl, whisk together the flour, sugar, baking powder, and salt.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the non-dairy milk, non-dairy yogurt, oil, vinegar (or lemon juice), and vanilla extract. Let sit for 2 minutes.
Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients and gently fold until just combined (do not overmix)!!
Gently fold in the blueberries. If using frozen blueberries, add them straight from the freezer (do not thaw).
Divide the batter evenly into the muffin cups, filling each almost to the top.
Bake for 18–22 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean (blueberry streaks are fine—just no wet batter).
Let the muffins cool in the pan for 5–10 minutes, then enjoy!

title: Oatmeal Raisin Cookie
description: Amazing Vegan Oatmeal Raisin Cookie! Makes about 20-24 cookies.
steps: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C) and line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
In a medium bowl, whisk together flour, oats, starch, baking powder, baking soda, and salt. Set bowl aside.
In a separate bowl, cream together the melted vegan butter, granulated sugar, and brown sugar for about 3 minutes.
Stir in the dairy-free yogurt/applesauce and vanilla extract into the sugar/butter mixture until combined.
Gradually mix the dry ingredients into the wet ingredients to form a dough. I recommend using a wooden spoon.
Fold in raisins.
Scoop the cookie dough onto the baking sheet, roughly the size of 2 tablespoons per cookie and bake for 10–12 minutes, or until edges are golden brown.
Let cookies cool for about 10 minutes and then enjoy :)

title: Oatmeal Chocolate Chip Cookie
description: Amazing Vegan Oatmeal Chocolate Chip Cookie! Makes about 20-24 cookies.
ingredients: 1 cup (220 g) vegan chocolate chips or chunks
steps: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C) and line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
In a medium bowl, whisk together flour, oats, starch, baking powder, baking soda, and salt. Set bowl aside.
In a separate bowl, cream together the melted vegan butter, granulated sugar, and brown sugar for about 3 minutes.
Stir in the dairy-free yogurt/applesauce and vanilla extract into the sugar/butter mixture until combined.
Gradually mix the dry ingredients into the wet ingredients to form a dough. I recommend using a wooden spoon.
Fold in chocolate chips or chunks.
Optional: Put the dough in the fridge for around 2 hours. This step isn't necesarry, but I've noticed the cookies do come out more flavorful and gooey when refigerated before baking.
Scoop the cookie dough onto the baking sheet, roughly the size of 2 tablespoons per cookie and bake for 10–12 minutes, or until edges are golden brown.
Let cookies cool for about 10 minutes and then enjoy :)

title: Lemon White Chocolate Chip Cookie
description: Amazing Lemon White Chocolate Chip Cookie! Makes about 24-28 cookies.
ingredients: 1 cup (226 g) cold salted vegan butter, cubed
1/3 cup (70 g) dairy free yogurt, cold
steps: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C) and line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
In a medium bowl, whisk together flour, cornstarch, baking soda, and salt. Set bowl aside.
In a seperate bowl, add in the brown sugar, white sugar, and lemon zest. Rub the lemon zest into the sugar to release the lemon extract.
In the same bowl, cream together the cold vegan butter, granulated sugar, brown sugar, and lemon zest for about 3 minutes.
Stir in the dairy-free yogurt/applesauce, vanilla extract, and lemon juice into the sugar/butter mixture until combined.
Gradually mix the dry ingredients into the wet ingredients to form a dough. I recommend using a wooden spoon.
Fold in white chocolate chips.
Optional: Put the dough in the fridge for around 2 hours. This step isn't necesarry, but I've noticed the cookies do come out more flavorful and gooey when refigerated before baking.
Scoop the cookie dough onto the baking sheet, roughly the size of 2 tablespoons per cookie and bake for 10–12 minutes, or until edges are golden brown.
Let cookies cool for about 10 minutes and then enjoy :)

title: Holiday Hot Cocoa Cookie
description: Amazing Holiday Hot Cocoa Cookie! Makes about 16-18 cookies.
ingredients: 1/2 tsp espresso powder (optional, but highly recommended)
1 cup (170 g) vegan chocolate chips or however many your heart desires :)
steps: Before you start, I highly recommend using a kitchen scale for measuring out the ingredients!
In a bowl, whisk together flour, cocoa powder, baking soda, salt, and espresso powder. Set this bowl aside.
In a separate bowl, cream the softened vegan butter with brown sugar and granulated sugar for around 3 minutes using a mixer. Slowly add in the vanilla and apple sauce until well combined.
Gradually add the dry ingredients to the wet, mixing with a wooden spoon gently until a dough forms.
Fold in the chocolate chips or chunks. I recommend using 2 types for a nice variety, Trader Joes has an excellent selection.
Optional: Put the dough in the fridge for around 2 hours. This step isn't necesarry, but I've noticed the cookies do come out more flavorful and gooey when refigerated before baking.
When creating the dough balls, place a peice of marshmallow inside the ball and seal closed.
Scoop the cookie dough onto the baking sheet, roughly the size of 2 tablespoons per cookie and bake for 10–12 minutes, or until you can tell they are done!
Let cookies cool for about 10 minutes and then enjoy :)

title: Strawberry ShortCake
description: A stunning vegan Strawberry Shortcake layered with fluffy vanilla cake, strawberry preserves swirled right into the batter, dreamy Chantilly cream, and fresh strawberries. This one is a showstopper and nobody will ever guess it's plant-based! Serves about 8-10.
ingredients: 1 tbsp white vinegar or lemon juice
2 cups vegan heavy whipping cream (Silk recommended)
6-7 strawberries, tops removed and sliced in half
steps: Prepare two 8" round cake pans by greasing them and inserting a parchment circle into the bottom of each. Preheat oven to 350°F.
In a medium bowl, combine flour, baking powder, baking soda, and salt. Whisk to combine and set aside.
In a large bowl, combine granulated sugar, non-dairy milk, melted vegan butter, vinegar, and vanilla. Whisk well to combine. Then add the dry ingredients and whisk to form the cake batter.
Divide the cake batter evenly between the two prepared pans. If you have a kitchen scale, it will be about 20 ounces of batter per pan.
Top each pan of batter with 3 tablespoons of strawberry preserves, distributing evenly. Use a knife to gently swirl the preserves into the batter.
Bake at 350°F for 31-33 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean. Allow the cake layers to cool completely before assembling.

To make the Chantilly Cream: add the vegan heavy cream to a stand mixer fitted with a whisk attachment (or use a hand mixer) and whisk on medium speed for about 30 seconds. Add the powdered sugar and vanilla, then continue mixing on medium-high until stiff peaks form.

To assemble: place one cake layer on your serving plate. Spread a generous layer of Chantilly cream on top, then arrange the halved strawberries around the edge and scatter the chopped strawberries in the center.
Place the second cake layer on top and frost with the remaining Chantilly cream. Garnish with the additional strawberries on top. Slice and enjoy!

title: Lemon Cake
description: A bright and zesty vegan Lemon Cake layered with homemade lemon curd and a fluffy lemon buttercream frosting. This one is light, fresh, and absolutely bursting with lemon flavor — perfect for spring or honestly any time you need a little sunshine!
ingredients: 1 1/4 cups unsweetened soy milk or almond milk
1/2 cup applesauce (I use this instead of aquafaba!)
8 tbsp (1/2 cup) vegan butter, softened to room temperature
1/2 cup neutral flavored oil (vegetable or grapeseed)
1/4 tsp turmeric or a few drops of yellow food coloring (optional)
1 cup vegan butter, softened to room temperature
steps: Preheat oven to 350°F. Grease two 8" round cake pans and line the bottoms with parchment rounds to prevent sticking.
Make the vegan buttermilk: combine the soy milk and apple cider vinegar in a measuring cup. Let sit for a few minutes until it curdles. Set aside.

Make the lemon curd: add all lemon curd ingredients to a small pot and warm over medium heat, whisking frequently until the mixture thickens, about 5-10 minutes. It will still be thin when warm — refrigerate for a few hours or overnight for a thicker curd.

In a large bowl, beat the softened vegan butter, oil, sugar, and lemon zest on medium speed until light and fluffy, about 3 minutes. Add the lemon extract, vanilla extract, and food coloring (if using) and beat to combine.

On low speed, mix in the vegan buttermilk and applesauce. Add the flour, baking powder, and salt on top and mix on low until just combined, about 1-2 minutes. Scrape the sides as needed and do not over mix!
Divide the batter evenly between the two prepared pans. Bake for 30-35 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean.
Let the cakes cool on wire racks for 5-10 minutes, then run a knife around the edges, carefully invert onto your hand, remove the parchment, and place on a wire rack to cool completely.

Make the buttercream: beat the vegan butter until light and fluffy, about 2 minutes. Add 2 cups of powdered sugar, lemon juice, lemon zest, and vanilla. Beat on low then switch to medium until fluffy. Add the remaining 2 cups of powdered sugar and mix. Add non-dairy milk a tablespoon at a time if too thick, or more powdered sugar if too thin.

To assemble: place one cake layer on your plate or cake stand and spread an even layer of lemon curd and buttercream on top. Place the second layer on top, then frost the top and sides with the remaining buttercream. Decorate as desired and enjoy!

title: Chocolate Raspberry Cake
description: A rich and decadent vegan Chocolate Raspberry Cake with layers of deep, fudgy chocolate cake, a fresh raspberry filling, and a silky chocolate buttercream. This one is seriously impressive and tastes even better than it looks!
ingredients: 1 1/4 cups (300 ml) vegan buttermilk, room temperature
1 cup (95 g) dutch-process cocoa powder, sifted
1/2 cup (113 g) unsalted vegan butter, melted and cooled
1 cup (240 ml) hot water + 1 tsp espresso powder (or 1 cup brewed coffee, decaf if desired)
2 cups (460 g) vegan butter, room temperature
1/4 - 1/3 cup dairy-free milk (soy or oat milk recommended)
steps: Preheat oven to 350°F. Grease two or three 8" round cake pans and line the bottoms with parchment rounds.
Make the vegan buttermilk: combine non-dairy milk with a splash of apple cider vinegar and let sit for a few minutes until it curdles. Set aside.
In a large bowl, sift together the flour, cocoa powder, baking powder, baking soda, and salt. Whisk to combine and set aside.
In a separate large bowl, whisk together the granulated sugar, brown sugar, melted vegan butter, applesauce, and vanilla extract until smooth and well combined.
Add the vegan buttermilk to the wet mixture and whisk to combine. Gradually add the dry ingredients and mix until just combined — do not over mix!
Stir in the hot coffee or hot water with espresso powder. The batter will be thin, that's perfectly normal!
Divide the batter evenly between the prepared pans. Bake for 30-35 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean.
Let the cakes cool in the pans for 10 minutes, then run a knife around the edges and turn out onto a wire rack to cool completely before frosting.

Make the chocolate buttercream: beat the vegan butter in a stand mixer or with a hand mixer until fluffy and light, about 2-3 minutes. Add the cocoa powder and 1 cup of powdered sugar and cream together. Continue adding the powdered sugar in 1 cup intervals until smooth. Add dairy-free milk 1-2 tablespoons at a time to reach a creamy, spreadable consistency.

To assemble: place one cake layer on your plate or cake stand. Spread a layer of chocolate buttercream and add fresh or jam-style raspberry filling on top. Place the next layer and repeat. Frost the top and sides with the remaining buttercream, decorate with fresh raspberries, and enjoy!

title: Lemon Cupcakes
description: Bright, fluffy vegan Lemon Cupcakes filled with homemade lemon curd and topped with a silky lemon buttercream. This recipe makes about 20-24 standard cupcakes, or 12 extra large bakery-style ones if you fill them to the brim!
ingredients: 1 1/4 cups unsweetened soy milk or almond milk
8 tbsp (1/2 cup) vegan butter, softened to room temperature
1/2 cup neutral flavored oil (vegetable or grapeseed)
1/4 tsp turmeric or a few drops of yellow food coloring (optional)
1 cup vegan butter, softened to room temperature
steps: Preheat oven to 350°F. Line two standard muffin tins with cupcake liners. This recipe makes about 20-24 standard cupcakes, or 12 extra large ones.
Make the vegan buttermilk: combine the soy milk and apple cider vinegar in a measuring cup. Let sit for a few minutes until it curdles. Set aside.

Make the lemon curd: add all lemon curd ingredients to a small pot and warm over medium heat, whisking frequently until thickened, about 5-10 minutes. It will still be thin when warm — refrigerate for a few hours for a thicker curd.

In a large bowl, beat the softened vegan butter, oil, sugar, and lemon zest on medium speed until light and fluffy, about 3 minutes. Add the lemon extract, vanilla, and food coloring (if using) and beat to combine.
On low speed, mix in the vegan buttermilk and applesauce. Add the flour, baking powder, and salt and mix on low until just combined, about 1-2 minutes. Scrape the sides as needed — do not over mix!
Fill each cupcake liner about 2/3 full. Bake for 18-22 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean. Let cool completely before filling and frosting.

Make the buttercream: beat the vegan butter until light and fluffy, about 2 minutes. Add 2 cups of powdered sugar, lemon juice, lemon zest, and vanilla. Beat on low then switch to medium until fluffy. Add the remaining 2 cups of powdered sugar and mix. Add non-dairy milk a tablespoon at a time if too thick.
To fill the cupcakes: use a small knife or cupcake corer to cut a small hole in the center of each cooled cupcake. Spoon or pipe a small amount of lemon curd into each hole.
Pipe or spread the lemon buttercream on top of each cupcake, garnish with a little lemon zest or a slice of lemon, and enjoy!
